community.welcomeLead: Start here for a clear picture of who we serve, how the board helps, and what to expect in your first weeks.
community.missionSnapshot: We strengthen neighborhoods through programs and partnerships. The board keeps mission and community trust at the center while supporting staff who run day-to-day operations.

orientationTimeline.title: Organization overview
orientationTimeline.summary: Mission, leadership, and how programs reach families. Skim the handbook and bylaws index.

orientationTimeline.title: Governance basics
orientationTimeline.summary: Fiduciary role, committees, and how the board partners with the executive director.

orientationTimeline.title: Policies and meetings
orientationTimeline.summary: Expectations, conflicts of interest, and how packets and votes work before your first full meeting.

governanceBasics.heading: Fiduciary duties
governanceBasics.body: Act in the organizations best interest, stay informed, and ask questions when something is unclear.

governanceBasics.heading: Board vs staff
governanceBasics.body: The board sets direction, approves major decisions, and oversees health and compliance.

governanceBasics.heading: Oversight
governanceBasics.body: You review finances, major risks, and executive performance through a governance lens.

governanceBasics.heading: Meetings
governanceBasics.body: Read the packet ahead of time, join committees you care about, and share context from the community you represent.

quickAnswers.question: What is the board responsible for?
quickAnswers.answer: Mission, strategy, financial health, executive oversight, and governance policies.

quickAnswers.question: Where do I find meeting materials?
quickAnswers.answer: Board packets are shared before each meeting. Orientation PDFs live under Resources below.

quickAnswers.question: How are votes handled?
quickAnswers.answer: Most formal votes happen in meetings after discussion. The Voting area shows timing when used.

quickAnswers.question: Who do I contact with questions?
quickAnswers.answer: Start with the board chair or governance committee lead.

resources.title: Board handbook
resources.description: Roles, meeting norms, and how to stay engaged between meetings.

resources.title: Bylaws
resources.description: Legal structure: officers, meetings, and amendment process.

resources.title: Conflict of interest policy
resources.description: When to disclose and how the board manages conflicts.

resources.title: Strategic plan (summary)
resources.description: Three-year priorities and how programs connect to the mission.

resources.title: Recent board packet
resources.description: Latest agenda, reports, and materials for the upcoming meeting.

resources.title: Orientation packet (PDF)
resources.description: Printable welcome packet for new members.

modules.title: Welcome to the organization
modules.summary: Mission, leadership, and current priorities in plain language.
content: You will see how we describe our work to neighbors, partners, and donors.
Meet the leadership team and how committees connect to programs you care about.

modules.title: Your role as a board member
modules.summary: What the board does, what it does not do, and how volunteers fit in here.
content: In a volunteer-heavy organization, clarity between governance and operations matters.
Recruitment and onboarding are board-led.

modules.title: Governance basics
modules.summary: Fiduciary duties, oversight, and working with the executive director.
content: Focus on the big picture: finances, risk, and mission alignment.
Ask for what you need to feel prepared.

modules.title: Key policies and expectations
modules.summary: Conduct, confidentiality, and conflict standards in everyday terms.
content: Policies protect the organization and the people we serve.
You will know when to step back from a decision where you have a personal interest.

modules.title: Board meetings and decisions
modules.summary: Packets, agendas, discussion, and how decisions get recorded.
content: We value discussion before votes; consent agendas handle routine items.
Minutes capture decisions, not every comment.

modules.title: Important documents
modules.summary: Bylaws, plan, handbook—what to skim first.
content: You do not need to memorize everything; know where to find answers.
Orientation packet is optional but helpful before your first meeting.

modules.title: Current priorities and strategic direction
modules.summary: What the board and staff are focused on this year.
content: Tie what you read here to committee work and community listening.
Strategic refresh may surface in meetings.

growing.welcomeLead: Welcome to a growing, multi-program organization. This hub orients you to oversight at scale and how we align as a board.
growing.missionSnapshot: We deliver services across education, workforce, and health. The board balances ambition with risk, clarity between programs, and a strong partnership with leadership.

orientationTimeline.title: Mission and portfolio
orientationTimeline.summary: How programs fit together and what the board sees in the packet each month.

orientationTimeline.title: Governance and reporting
orientationTimeline.summary: Unified outcomes reporting, committee structure, and ED-board cadence.

orientationTimeline.title: Policies and decisions
orientationTimeline.summary: Conflicts, confidentiality, and how major votes and resolutions are tracked.

governanceBasics.heading: Fiduciary duties
governanceBasics.body: With multiple programs, your job is to ask whether the whole enterprise stays solvent, compliant, and mission-true.

governanceBasics.heading: Board vs staff
governanceBasics.body: Leadership runs operations; the board approves strategy, budget, and policy.

governanceBasics.heading: Oversight
governanceBasics.body: Finance, audit, quality, and risk committees channel deep work; the full board focuses on direction and guardrails.

governanceBasics.heading: Meeting expectations
governanceBasics.body: Packets are longer than a small shop; skim the exec summary first, then go deeper on your committee lane.

quickAnswers.question: What is the board responsible for?
quickAnswers.answer: Mission, multi-year strategy, financial sustainability, CEO oversight, and governance policies across all programs.

quickAnswers.question: Where do I find meeting materials?
quickAnswers.answer: Portal under Documents and the monthly board packet. Committee leads may add pre-reads mid-month.

quickAnswers.question: How are votes handled?
quickAnswers.answer: Major items are on the agenda with backup; some approvals use written consent between meetings.

quickAnswers.question: Who do I contact with questions?
quickAnswers.answer: Board chair or chief governance lead for process; ED office for program context.

resources.title: Board handbook
resources.description: Governance calendar, committee charters, and norms at scale.

resources.title: Bylaws
resources.description: Board structure, officer roles, and meeting rules.

resources.title: Strategic plan
resources.description: Cross-program goals, metrics, and risk themes.

resources.title: Recent board packet
resources.description: Unified leadership briefing and committee reports.

resources.title: Orientation slide deck
resources.description: Recorded orientation and slides for async review.

modules.title: Welcome to the organization
modules.summary: Mission, leadership team, and how programs connect.
content: You will see how we talk about impact across sites and grant portfolios.
Meet the senior team and how information flows to the board.

modules.title: Your role as a board member
modules.summary: Oversight at scale: what good governance looks like here.
content: You help leadership prioritize where to invest attention and capital.
Recruitment looks for finance, programs, and community representation together.

modules.title: Governance basics
modules.summary: Fiduciary duties, reporting, and board-staff alignment.
content: Standardized reporting helps compare programs without drowning in detail.
Executive session is used thoughtfully for personnel and sensitive matters.

modules.title: Key policies and expectations
modules.summary: Conduct, confidentiality, data use, and conflicts.
content: Multi-site work means clear rules for data and partner agreements.
When unsure, disclose early.

modules.title: Board meetings and decisions
modules.summary: Agenda design, consent items, and decision logs.
content: Expect a mix of strategic blocks and standing approvals.
Resolutions and minutes tie back to the compliance calendar.

modules.title: Important documents
modules.summary: Handbook, bylaws, plan, and orientation media.
content: Use the handbook table of contents rather than reading cover to cover in one sitting.
Optional deck reinforces what you see live in orientation.

modules.title: Current priorities and strategic direction
modules.summary: Expansion, reporting maturity, and leadership structure.
content: Current board conversations focus on program growth and leadership clarity.
Your questions help stress-test assumptions before big commitments.

privateSchool.welcomeLead: Welcome to the board of St. Mark's Academy. This orientation connects mission, families, and the school's governance role.
privateSchool.missionSnapshot: We provide faith-based education with academic rigor. The board supports the head of school, safeguards mission and trust, and thinks about long-term sustainability.

orientationTimeline.title: School overview
orientationTimeline.summary: Mission, formation, enrollment, and how families experience the school.

orientationTimeline.title: Governance and leadership
orientationTimeline.summary: Board vs head of school, policy oversight, and committee rhythm.

orientationTimeline.title: Policies and community trust
orientationTimeline.summary: Handbook, safety, tuition and aid, and public-facing decisions.

governanceBasics.heading: Fiduciary duties
governanceBasics.body: You steward the mission, finances, and reputation of the school in the eyes of families, parish or sponsors, and regulators.

governanceBasics.heading: Board vs school leadership
governanceBasics.body: The head of school runs daily operations; the board hires, evaluates, and supports that leader and sets major policy.

governanceBasics.heading: Oversight
governanceBasics.body: Focus on safety, finance, advancement, and educational outcomes through board-appropriate dashboards.

governanceBasics.heading: Meetings and community
governanceBasics.body: Expect transparent processes for handbook and calendar decisions; confidentiality still matters for personnel and families.

quickAnswers.question: What is the board responsible for?
quickAnswers.answer: Mission, head-of-school oversight, major policy and finance, facilities strategy, and preserving community trust.

quickAnswers.question: Where do I find meeting materials?
quickAnswers.answer: Board portal and monthly packet; advancement and facilities committees may add focused pre-reads.

quickAnswers.question: How are votes handled?
quickAnswers.answer: Tuition, handbook, and capital items are typically board votes after committee recommendation.

quickAnswers.question: Who do I contact with questions?
quickAnswers.answer: Board chair or governance liaison; head of school office for school operations.

resources.title: Board handbook
resources.description: Board-school partnership, committees, and public communication norms.

resources.title: Conflict of interest policy
resources.description: Family relationships, vendors, and parish connections.

resources.title: Strategic plan
resources.description: Enrollment, campus, advancement, and formation priorities.

resources.title: Parent and family handbook (summary)
resources.description: What families see; aligns board policy work to daily school life.

modules.title: Welcome to the organization
modules.summary: Mission, leadership, and how the school serves families.
content: Formation and academics are both part of how we define success.
You will see how enrollment and parish or sponsor relationships shape planning.

modules.title: Your role as a board member
modules.summary: Policy oversight, head-of-school partnership, and community trust.
content: You are an ambassador for the mission without managing faculty or families directly.
Board decisions should be explainable to the community we serve.

modules.title: Governance basics
modules.summary: Fiduciary duties in a school context and delegation to leadership.
content: Know the difference between governance questions and operational ones before meetings.
Evaluation of the head of school is structured and confidential.

modules.title: Key policies and expectations
modules.summary: Handbook lines, safety, tuition and aid, conduct.
content: Policies balance compassion with consistency for hundreds of families.
Conflicts involving your family should be disclosed early.

modules.title: Board meetings and decisions
modules.summary: Agendas, committees, and how major school decisions move.
content: Facilities and advancement often have dedicated committee paths before the full board.
Minutes reflect approvals and key direction, especially on policy.

modules.title: Important documents
modules.summary: Bylaws, strategic plan, board and family handbooks.
content: Reading the family handbook summary helps you connect policy to parent experience.
Campus master plan excerpts appear in packets during capital seasons.

modules.title: Current priorities and strategic direction
modules.summary: Enrollment, facilities phases, scholarships, and parent engagement.
content: Current themes include sustainable tuition and aid, phased facilities work, and clear messaging in a competitive market.
Your questions help leadership test tradeoffs before public commitments.
